rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
Treatment and dose content was not mapped.
The exact source excludes children and people who are or were recently pregnant.
population_applicability: Partial: exact for non-pregnant people aged 16 years and over with suspected sepsis; not exact for children or pregnancy.
setting_applicability: Direct for recognition, assessment, transfer, monitoring, and source investigation; treatment remains clinician-led.
UAE_applicability: International NICE guidance requires UAE clinician review and local ambulance, emergency, microbiology, and referral pathways.
recency_verification: The current NICE NG253 recommendations and official PDF, including the 2026 wording update, were opened on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: NICE NG253 is the current official guideline for this exact scope.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped suspected-infection, fever, source, duration, and rigors fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports suspected-infection and fever presentation documentation, source symptoms, risk factors, and recognition that sepsis can present with nonspecific illness.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped observations, source examination, urine-output, and general-assessment fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports temperature, heart rate, respiratory rate, blood pressure, consciousness, oxygen saturation, skin signs, urine output, fever or rigors, and examination for the suspected infection source.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped confusion, hypotension, respiratory, rash, immunocompromise, and oliguria warning fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports confusion, respiratory compromise, hypotension, tachycardia, oliguria, temperature abnormality, non-blanching or mottled skin, and immunosuppression as assessed risk features.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped escalation, return, and warning-information documentation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports emergency transfer or escalation for high-risk presentations and documented monitoring, warning signs, and how to seek care for lower-risk presentations.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped blood, microbiology, urine, chest-X-ray, and imaging review.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-selected blood and microbiology testing, urinalysis, chest radiography, and abdominal or pelvic imaging to identify an infection source.

unresolved_source_gaps: Children, pregnancy, neck stiffness, broad “temperature” matching, hydration assessment, and the history draft containing neck-stiffness text remain unsupported.
Supportive care, hydration advice, antipyretics, antibiotics, and fixed follow-up remain unsupported and clinician-led.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
